title: User Access Management Audit
objective: Assess whether user access provisioning, deprovisioning, privileged access, and periodic access review controls are designed and operating effectively.

controls.name: User Deprovisioning Within 24 Hours
controls.description: Terminated users should be disabled promptly based on HR termination records.

controls.name: Privileged Access Ownership
controls.description: Administrative accounts should have named ownership and formal approval.

controls.name: Quarterly Access Review
controls.description: Business owners should review access rights for critical systems each quarter.

controls.name: MFA for Administrator Accounts
controls.description: All privileged accounts should be enrolled in multi-factor authentication.

evidence.title: HR Termination List
evidence.content: Termination report dated March 1 shows John Doe exited the company effective March 1. HR notes confirm offboarding ticket was raised the same day.

evidence.title: Active User Export
evidence.content: Identity export taken March 8 shows John Doe account remains enabled in Microsoft 365 and VPN groups.

evidence.title: Admin Account List
evidence.content: Administrative accounts inventory includes shared account admin_global with no named owner, no approval ticket, and no expiration date.

evidence.title: Access Review PDF
evidence.content: Q1 access certification covers ERP and payroll, but finance application Falcon lacks a business-owner signoff for the quarter.

evidence.title: MFA Settings Screenshot
evidence.content: Security dashboard screenshot shows administrator account svc-fin-admin is excluded from MFA because of a legacy integration issue.

evidence.title: Access Control Policy
evidence.content: Access Control Policy version 1.4 was last approved 27 months ago. The review cycle in the footer states policy owners must review annually.

issues.title: Failure to Deprovision User
issues.description: A terminated user remained active in core systems after departure.
issues.recommendation: Implement a daily reconciliation between HR terminations and access disablement.

issues.title: Shared Admin Account Without Ownership
issues.description: A privileged account exists without formal ownership or accountability.
issues.recommendation: Assign named ownership and remove shared privileged accounts where possible.

issues.title: Missing Quarterly Access Review
issues.description: A critical finance application lacked evidence of quarterly user access review.
issues.recommendation: Require business-owner certification for all critical systems each quarter.

issues.title: MFA Gap for Privileged Account
issues.description: One administrator account was exempted from MFA.
issues.recommendation: Remediate technical blockers and enforce MFA for all privileged accounts.

issues.title: Outdated Access Control Policy
issues.description: The access control policy was not reviewed according to the defined annual cycle.
issues.recommendation: Establish policy ownership and a formal annual review cadence.

inbox.subject: Kickoff: User Access Management Audit
inbox.preview: Use this week to determine whether offboarding and admin access controls are actually operating.
inbox.body: Welcome to your first fieldwork sprint. Focus on access provisioning and deprovisioning, privileged account ownership, quarterly reviews, and MFA coverage. Gather enough support to determine whether controls are not only documented, but operating in practice.

inbox.subject: Requested extracts attached
inbox.preview: I've exported the latest user and admin lists. Let me know if you need anything else.
inbox.body: Attached are the current user export and administrator inventory. We believe offboarding is generally handled same-day, though some handoffs depend on HR timing. Happy to walk through the process if needed.
